channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: From first lady to president? Inside the rise of Peru’s Keiko Fujimori
item.description: In her fourth bid to be president, Fujimori faces her best odds yet. But can she overcome scandal and her father's past?

item.title: ‘Unacceptable situation’: Minister cries foul as Jamaica suffers blackout
item.description: Minister Daryl Vaz reassures residents that power had been restored across Jamaica after a rare islandwide outage.

item.title: Why are Armenia’s elections being so keenly watched abroad?
item.description: Russia, the United States and European Union all have interests as geopolitics take centre-stage.

item.title: 100 days into the war on Iran, Trump fails to rally US support
item.description: Unpopularity of the war may affect the US approach to the conflict and hurt Republicans in the elections, analysts say.

item.title: Which sub-Saharan Africa sides have best World Cup chance? Senegal, Ghana?
item.description: After Morocco became the first Africans to reach a World Cup semifinal in 2022, can a sub-Saharan side step up in 2026?

item.title: Russian teenager Mirra Andreeva wins French Open to claim first Grand Slam
item.description: Eighth-ranked Mirra Andreeva ended the run of 114th-ranked Polish qualifier Maja Chwalinska in 6-3, 6-2 win in final.

item.title: Activists disrupt German military exhibit over arms sales to Israel
item.description: Activists disrupt German military exhibit over arms sales to Israel.

item.title: Israeli attacks in Lebanon kill 10 people, including high-ranking soldiers
item.description: The deaths include a brigadier general, a captain and a soldier killed while travelling on the Khardali-Nabatieh road.

item.title: ‘I’m a cockroach’: Gen Z protest movement lands in Indian capital
item.description: India's Gen Z Cockroach movement stages its first protest in the capital, demanding education minister's resignation.

item.title: Videos show Kuwait intercepting ballistic missiles from Iran
item.description: Kuwait says it intercepted seven ballistic missiles over residential areas, resulting in some debris but no casualties.

item.title: Israeli attack on Gaza City tent camp kills at least six
item.description: Latest Israeli attack comes as Hamas readies for meetings in Egypt on full implementation of Gaza 'ceasefire' agreement.

item.title: What Afghanistan’s rotten apples tell us about its non-profit sector
item.description: Funding has dwindled, while need has grown. And yet NGO work continues to be plagued by inefficiency.

item.title: Lebanon and Israel’s perpetual war machine
item.description: Why Lebanon remains central to Israel's multi-front wars.

item.title: Pope calls for end to polarisation during visit to Spain
item.description: Huge gatherings are expected in the coming days for the first visit to Spain by a pope since 2011.

item.title: ‘Elections campaigned on who can be more brutal to Palestinians’
item.description: ‘Elections campaigned on who can be more brutal to Palestinians’

item.title: Argentina’s ‘Madman’: Inside the world of Javier Milei
item.description: Argentina’s self-styled anarcho-capitalist president impressed – and later alienated – many voters.

item.title: Pope Leo praises Spain’s ‘commitment to peace and solidarity’
item.description: Pope Leo XIV arrived in Madrid and was welcomed by King Felipe VI and Queen Letizia.

item.title: Hegseth says Europe is being ‘invaded by dangerous migrants’
item.description: Hegseth says Europe is being ‘invaded by dangerous migrants’.

item.title: Rescuers stop search for last two men lost in Laos cave
item.description: The search and rescue team say it is 'high risk' to continue the operation.

item.title: Groom killed hours before his wedding in Gaza
item.description: Muhannad Farwana never got to wear his wedding suit, Israel killed him in an air strike hours before his wedding.

item.title: Hundreds brave freezing weather in La Paz to line up for affordable food
item.description: Hundreds of Bolivian residents are braving near-freezing temperatures to queue for affordable chicken in La Paz.

item.title: Spain at World Cup 2026: Players to watch, group matches and squad
item.description: Led by some outrageously young and talented players, are Spain set to repeat another Euro-World Cup double in 2026?

item.title: Iran 2026 World Cup squad heads to Mexico as US visa dispute erupts
item.description: Iranian officials say a 'large' number of key staff denied visas as the team is set to travel to Mexico.

item.title: Russia says hundreds of Ukrainian drones downed as economic forum ends
item.description: Russia's defence ministry said the drones were downed over 16 areas and regions, including Crimea.

item.title: ‘Cockroach Party’ leader returns to India for first New Delhi protest
item.description: 'Cockroach Party' leader returns to India for first New Delhi protest.
